start.npcText: Eighteen. Your mother says it like a verdict, then can't hold the face and beams. Your father slides the wrapped bundle across the table. "The museum needs new stories, love. Nobody finds those at home. Go and adventure — bring us back something worth a placard. Bring yourself back happier than you left."

responses.text: "Do people even care about old things anymore?"
"I'll do it. I'll put us back on the map."

doubt.npcText: Your father looks at the empty hall through the doorway for a long moment. "They care. They've just forgotten they care — too busy scrying whatever everyone else is scrying. You can't argue someone out of forgetting. But you can hand them one true thing with a story attached, and watch."

responses.text: "Then I'll find them one true thing. To start."
